domain:
In an e-commerce retail organization, customers are registered with unique ids to place orders that includes multiple products. An order also has a unique identifier and consists of one or more products, which are described by their name and description. When the customer completes the order, it is necessary to specify the quantity requested and the price for the item is updated.

The retailer also ask customers to provide feedback by writing reviews for specific products. These reviews capture the opinion of a customer regarding a product, including the date it was written and a rating score. Finally, the marketing department organizes promotional campaigns. A product may be included in various promotions over time. Whenever a product is part of a campaign, specific terms are established for that offer, such as the applicable discount rate and a validity period defined by a start and end date.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Promotion and Offer be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Offer would depend on Promotion for its existence.
2. Use association, Offer could exist independently of Promotion, and can also be linked to multiple promotions.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Offer depends on Promotion.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Offer exists independently of Promotion.
questions.selected: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Offer exists independently of Promotion.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Promotion in the relationship with Offer?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an offer can exist without any associated promotion.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an offer must have at least one associated promotion.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Offer can exist without Promotion.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Offer must have at least one Promotion.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Offer can exist without Promotion.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Offer in the relationship with Promotion?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a promotion can exist without any associated offer.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a promotion must have at least one associated offer.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Promotion can exist without Offer.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Promotion must have at least one Offer.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Promotion must have at least one Offer.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Offer in the relationship with Promotion?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a promotion is associated with exactly one offer.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a promotion can be associated with multiple offers.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each promotion links to one offer.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each promotion can link to multiple offers.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each promotion can link to multiple offers.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 2: Many as maximum cardinality, each promotion can link to multiple offers.

This option is better because the domain description states that a product may be included in various promotions over time, implying that promotions can encompass multiple offers.

questions.question:
Should OrderItem be modeled as an association class linking Product and Order, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: OrderItem exists only as part of the relationship between Product and Order. A order item cannot exist without the link between Product and Order.
2. Regular class: OrderItem is a class that has links to Product and Order. In this case multiple order items can exist for every Product and Order.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, OrderItem as association class linking Product and Order.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, OrderItem as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, OrderItem as association class linking Product and Order.

questions.question:
Should Review be modeled as an association class linking Customer and Product, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Review exists only as part of the relationship between Customer and Product. A review cannot exist without the link between Customer and Product.
2. Regular class: Review is a class that has links to Customer and Product. In this case multiple reviews can exist for every Customer and Product.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Review as association class linking Customer and Product.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Review as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 2: Regular class, Review as separate class with two associations.

questions.question:
Should Offer be modeled as an association class linking Product and Promotion, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Offer exists only as part of the relationship between Product and Promotion. A offer cannot exist without the link between Product and Promotion.
2. Regular class: Offer is a class that has links to Product and Promotion. In this case multiple offers can exist for every Product and Promotion.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Offer as association class linking Product and Promotion.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Offer as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Offer as association class linking Product and Promotion.
